heroHeadline: Al-Shabaab Mortars Halane Camp Near Mogadishu Airport; AU Forces Killed — 16 Days to Presidential Legitimacy Cliff
heroSubtitle: Al-Shabaab conducted a mortar attack on Halane base camp — adjacent to Aden Adde International Airport and housing AUSSOM HQ, UN agencies, and Western diplomatic missions — on April 28, 2026, killing AU soldiers in one of the most audacious strikes against Mogadishu's international security zone in months. The attack came alongside an SNA operation seizing an Al-Shabaab hideout in Middle Shabelle, demonstrating simultaneous offensive and defensive pressure across multiple fronts. The constitutional crisis is now entering its final 16 days before President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's May 15 term expiry, with Horn Review's April 28 analysis documenting the full scope of the legitimacy gap: parliamentary mandate expired April 14 (Puntland declared constitutional vacuum April 17), the FGS constitutional amendments are disputed as lacking required quorum, and opposition leader groups including former President Sharif Sheikh Ahmed and Jubbaland President Ahmed Madobe have threatened a parallel election within one month if dialogue is refused. Puntland President Deni has warned the FGS 'will no longer be considered in existence' after May 15. At sea, the Honour 25 fuel tanker with 17 multinational crew enters Day 4 of pirate captivity off Puntland, with the captain's family publicly pleading for international assistance and Al-Monitor identifying the perpetrators as a newly-formed pirate group of rural youth — while DefenceWeb reports a second vessel has been hijacked, signaling a broader resurgence. Somalia's humanitarian crisis is compounded further by Iran-linked shipping disruptions in the Red Sea delaying therapeutic food for malnourished children by 6+ weeks and raising transport costs 50%, per a Middle East Observer analysis published April 29.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, EU EPF, EU NAVFOR, Combined Maritime Forces, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
